The '''Sargesso asteroids'''<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> were situated in the [[Sargesso system/Legends|Sargesso system]], a part of the [[Tharin sector/Legends|Tharin sector]]<ref name="EAOC Sargesso" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC Sargesso" /> After escaping from<ref name="Prima" /> the [[Oovo system/Legends|Oovo system's]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]] [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Oovo IV/Legends|Oovo IV]]<ref name="Prima" /> at some point by [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="Date">[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] describes [[Meeko Ghintee]] leading a [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Force]] [[Longshot (Tri-Mark VII Interceptor)|Tri-Mark VII Interceptor]] into an ambush in the Sargesso [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]]. {{CSWECite|I|338|Ghintee, Meeko}} establishes that Ghintee was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] during the events of ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> the criminal [[Meeko Ghintee]] was pursued by a [[Tri-Mark VII Interceptor]] of the [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Force]]. Ghintee then led the [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] into an ambush in the Sargesso [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroids]]. There, the criminal rammed the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]], breaching its [[Hull/Legends|hull]], destroying its [[Life support/Legends|life support systems]], and [[Death/Legends|killing]] its crew. Ghintee later repaired and refitted the ship, naming it the [[Longshot (Tri-Mark VII Interceptor)|''Longshot'']], and eventually came to reside on the [[Outland Transit Station]]<ref name="Prima" /> of the [[Baxel sector/Legends|Baxel sector]].<ref name="EAOC Outland">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for the [[Outland Transit Station system|star system]] housing the [[Outland Transit Station]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Sargesso asteroids were introduced in [[Prima Games]]' [[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|Official Strategy Guide]] for<ref name="Prima" /> the [[November 19]], [[2002]]<ref name="SWBHdate">{{WebCite|url=http://www.gamefaqs.com/ps2/557901-star-wars-bounty-hunter/data|text=''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'' Release Information for PlayStation 2|work=[[Wikipedia:GameFAQs|GameFAQs]]|archivedate=20160304090346}}</ref> [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]''.<ref name="SWBH">''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]''</ref> The guide was authored by [[David Hodgson]]<ref name="Prima" /> and published on [[November 26]] of the same year.<ref name="Amazon">{{Amazon|url=Bounty-Hunter-Primas-Official-Strategy/dp/0761541640|text=Star Wars Bounty Hunter (Prima's Official Strategy Guide)}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Sargesso [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the asteroids, in grid square S-8.<ref name="TEA" />
